user_stories.action: submit Owner Intent & Request Intake from free-form text, media, and documents
user_stories.outcome: a Service Call Case Lifecycle record is created with reviewable structured context in one session

acceptance_criteria.description: Free-form intake creates a durable service case
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When a homeowner submits a new intake with at least one problem description, the system creates exactly one Intake Submission, one Intake Normalization, and one Service Case linked to the same property before the session ends

acceptance_criteria.description: Normalization output is reviewable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The created service case shows extracted issue type, urgency, location, and constraints as editable structured fields before the homeowner confirms submission

acceptance_criteria.description: Evidence is retained with the case
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each uploaded photo, video, file, or note appears as an Intake Evidence Item linked to the Service Case and remains retrievable from the case record after submission

user_stories.action: maintain structured property context for a property, unit, physical system, property asset, and property history event
user_stories.outcome: downstream maintenance and coordination workflows use persistent property context instead of re-collecting the same facts

acceptance_criteria.description: Property context can be created and enriched over time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A homeowner can create a Property with Address and later add or edit Unit, Physical System, Property Asset, Property Media, and Property History Event records without creating a duplicate property record

acceptance_criteria.description: Case creation reuses stored property context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When a new Service Case is opened for an existing property, the case can be linked to at least one existing Unit, Physical System, or Property Asset selected from stored property context

acceptance_criteria.description: Mandatory onboarding scope is configurable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The system enforces one configured onboarding rule set for property creation, with each required field visibly marked as required and each optional field accepted as deferred enrichment

user_stories.action: coordinate External Vendor Coordination for a service case
user_stories.outcome: provider options, outreach, estimates, and execution follow-up stay in one case record whether or not the provider is onboarded

acceptance_criteria.description: Provider recommendations are case-specific
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: For a service case with a defined trade and service location, the system can display one or more Match Recommendation records linked to the case and the recommended Service Provider records

acceptance_criteria.description: Off-platform vendor outreach is preserved
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each outreach attempt to a provider creates a Vendor Outreach record with timestamp, outreach channel, responsible actor, and linked service case

acceptance_criteria.description: Estimate collection supports configured launch mode
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The product can operate in exactly one configured Phase 1 estimate mode per tenant: provider matching only, or estimate collection with Estimate Request and Estimate records visible in the service case

user_stories.action: record provider and budget decisions in the Decision, Rationale & Trust Ledger
user_stories.outcome: material decisions are transparent, reviewable, and connected to their evidence

acceptance_criteria.description: Decision records preserve alternatives
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: For any service case decision marked material, the system stores one Decision Record with at least one Decision Option and a final outcome state of approved, rejected, or selected

acceptance_criteria.description: Rationale and evidence are required for material decisions
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A material decision cannot be marked final until the record includes one Decision Rationale entry and at least one Decision Evidence Link to an estimate, message, document, or case artifact

acceptance_criteria.description: Approvals are attributable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each approval, rejection, or acknowledgment on a material decision creates a Decision Approval record that stores actor identity, timestamp, and decision outcome

user_stories.action: track a service case from intake through completion in one workspace
user_stories.outcome: status, documents, communications, appointments, and closure history remain visible without leaving the platform

acceptance_criteria.description: Lifecycle stages are durable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each service case displays its current case stage and a complete Case Status History showing every stage change with actor and timestamp

acceptance_criteria.description: Case context is unified
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The case detail view can display linked property context, documents, message threads, appointments, estimates, and decision records from the same service case record

acceptance_criteria.description: Completion produces a retrievable closure record
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When a case is marked complete or closed, the system preserves closure date, final outcome, and all prior linked records, and the closed case remains searchable by property and issue type

user_stories.action: manage open work across multiple properties from one Property Portfolio Management workspace
user_stories.outcome: tenant issues, vendor coordination, and records are organized across several residences

acceptance_criteria.description: Portfolio workspace rolls up active work
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A landlord portfolio view lists open Service Case records across all linked properties and shows at minimum property, current status, priority, and latest update for each case

acceptance_criteria.description: Prioritization can be recorded across properties
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The landlord can create or update a Portfolio Priority Item tied to a property and service case, and the saved ranking is reflected in the portfolio work list

acceptance_criteria.description: Portfolio operating mode is explicit
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The product supports one configured portfolio mode per account type, either shared workflow behavior for small landlords and investors or differentiated workflow behavior with role-specific fields and views

user_stories.action: compare maintenance activity and outcomes across multiple properties
user_stories.outcome: repeatable decisions and durable historical records protect portfolio value

acceptance_criteria.description: Cross-property history is retrievable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: An investor can filter closed and open service cases by property, property asset, service provider, and issue type and retrieve matching records from the portfolio workspace

acceptance_criteria.description: Comparable portfolio metrics are available
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The system can generate a Portfolio Snapshot showing at least case count, total estimate value, total approved value, and completion count for a selected date range across portfolio properties

acceptance_criteria.description: Decision records remain attached to portfolio comparisons
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: From a cross-property comparison view, the investor can open the linked Decision Record and supporting evidence for each compared case without leaving the portfolio context

user_stories.action: report a rental maintenance issue and coordinate access
user_stories.outcome: the owner can remain responsible while the tenant can submit evidence, receive updates, and provide entry details

acceptance_criteria.description: Tenant intake does not require owner intervention at submission time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A tenant associated with a property occupancy record can create a new Intake Submission and linked Service Case without a prior owner approval action in the same session

acceptance_criteria.description: Occupancy and access details persist through the case
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The service case stores linked Property Occupancy and Access Requirement records, and both remain visible after scheduling and after completion

acceptance_criteria.description: Tenant sees permitted updates and appointment information
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When a case status changes or an appointment is scheduled, rescheduled, or canceled, the tenant can view the updated status and appointment window in their case view if their visibility role permits it

user_stories.action: grant and control delegated authority for a household delegate
user_stories.outcome: delegated representatives can act within defined scope while the owner retains approval control and attribution visibility

acceptance_criteria.description: Delegated authority is scoped
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: An owner can create a Delegated Authority record that specifies delegate identity, property scope, allowed actions, effective start date, and optional end date

acceptance_criteria.description: Out-of-scope actions are blocked
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: If a delegate attempts an action outside the granted scope, the system denies the action and records the denied attempt with actor, target record, and timestamp

acceptance_criteria.description: Approval thresholds route back to the owner
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: For any action type marked owner approval required, the delegate can submit the action for review but the final state remains pending until the owner records an approval or rejection

user_stories.action: facilitate complex service cases and escalations
user_stories.outcome: manual interventions, off-platform follow-up, and customer-visible updates remain in the same operational record

acceptance_criteria.description: Staff can access full case context before acting
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A staff case workspace shows linked intake details, property context, provider activity, message history, appointments, documents, and current case stage for the selected service case

acceptance_criteria.description: Manual interventions are captured durably
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each manual intervention creates either a Support Ticket, Escalation Record, Admin Action Log entry, or case note linked to the service case with actor and timestamp

acceptance_criteria.description: Customer-facing status is updated after staff action
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When staff changes the case stage or appointment state, the new status appears in the customer-visible case view and in Case Status History without requiring a second manual sync step

user_stories.action: configure the Phase 1 homeowner release mode for Service Call Case Lifecycle coverage and mediation policy
user_stories.outcome: the launch boundary is explicit and enforced instead of being implied by ad hoc operational practice

acceptance_criteria.description: Lifecycle scope is configurable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The platform stores exactly one active Phase 1 lifecycle scope mode per tenant, chosen from staff-assisted intake and matching only, or end-to-end service call management including scheduling and resolution

acceptance_criteria.description: Automation policy is configurable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The platform stores an active mediation policy that marks intake normalization, provider outreach, customer communication, and scheduling as either automated, staff-mediated, or mixed with rule-based routing

acceptance_criteria.description: Supported homeowner request types are explicit
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The platform stores one active launch taxonomy for homeowner request types, and intake submission only allows categories that belong to the configured set of urgent repairs, routine maintenance, planned improvements, or all supported categories

user_stories.action: manage contractor work from opportunity to scheduled job, execution, and invoice inside FSM
user_stories.outcome: customer, property, schedule, execution, and billing context stay connected through lead-to-cash operations

acceptance_criteria.description: Opportunity converts into scheduled work
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A provider can convert an accepted opportunity or estimate into a Job or Work Order with a scheduled Appointment linked to the same customer and property records

acceptance_criteria.description: Execution context is retained
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each job record can store Job Task, Job Material, dispatch assignment, appointment history, and completion notes without losing the original linked service case reference

acceptance_criteria.description: Billing is generated from completed work
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A completed job can generate an Invoice with one or more Invoice Line Item records tied to the same provider, property, and job

user_stories.action: run CAM operations from intake through governance-ready records
user_stories.outcome: resident requests, violations, approvals, vendor work, and correspondence remain connected to the right community context

acceptance_criteria.description: Community work is routed into one queue
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A manager workspace can display Community Work Queue Item records for at least resident requests, Violation Case records, ARC requests, and vendor work orders with current owner and status

acceptance_criteria.description: Community context is preserved
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each community work item can be linked to the relevant Community, Unit, owner or resident, vendor, governing document, and correspondence records from the same case or queue item

acceptance_criteria.description: Board escalation preserves support context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A manager can escalate a board-facing matter into a meeting or packet workflow while retaining all linked documents, recommendations, and prior correspondence

user_stories.action: review board matters and record approvals or votes with supporting rationale
user_stories.outcome: governance decisions are limited to board-relevant information and remain auditable

acceptance_criteria.description: Board access is scoped to governance materials
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A board member can open assigned Board Packet, Agenda Item, Vote, and supporting document records but cannot access operational records that are not linked to a board matter

acceptance_criteria.description: Votes and approvals are recorded with attribution
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each board vote or approval creates a Vote Response or Decision Approval record containing member identity, response value, and timestamp

acceptance_criteria.description: Manager recommendation is visible before action
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Before a board member records a vote or approval, the system displays the linked manager recommendation and supporting documents in the same board matter view

user_stories.action: oversee community operations across a management company portfolio
user_stories.outcome: standard workflows, metrics, and bottlenecks are visible across multiple associations and managers

acceptance_criteria.description: Portfolio metrics aggregate across communities
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A portfolio dashboard can show standardized KPI Snapshot or Portfolio Snapshot records across multiple communities for a selected reporting period

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow controls can be applied across communities
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A leadership user can assign a shared workflow template, policy, or setting to more than one community and the applied control becomes visible in each target community configuration

acceptance_criteria.description: Bottlenecks are filterable by operating dimension
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Leadership can filter open community work by manager, community, and workflow type and view counts for overdue or escalated items in each filter result

user_stories.action: use a resident portal to access notices, documents, requests, payments, and responsibilities
user_stories.outcome: association interactions are handled from one account-specific self-service experience

acceptance_criteria.description: Portal content is account-specific
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A resident portal shows only records linked to the resident's Portal Access Profile, including notices, documents, requests, charges, invoices, payments, and responsibility items

acceptance_criteria.description: Portal submissions route into the correct workflow
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A request, document upload, or payment initiated from the portal creates the corresponding operational record and links it to the correct community, unit, and resident account context

acceptance_criteria.description: Residents can complete required tasks
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: For any open Responsibility Item assigned to a resident, the portal exposes the required action state and records completion when the resident uploads the required document, acknowledges the notice, or makes the required payment

user_stories.action: track External HOA Context Tracking for work that may require HOA rules, approvals, notices, or governing documents
user_stories.outcome: HOA constraints remain visible even when the HOA is not a platform participant

acceptance_criteria.description: HOA-related constraints can be attached to a case
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A service case can store one or more Approval Requirement or Compliance Policy records tied to HOA rules, permit needs, or policy gates before work proceeds

acceptance_criteria.description: Governing documents are retrievable in context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A homeowner can link CC&Rs or other governing documents to the relevant property or service case and open the linked document from the case workspace

acceptance_criteria.description: Notice and approval actions are recorded
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each HOA-related submission, approval, denial, or notice creates an Approval Action or Correspondence Record linked to the same property and service case

user_stories.action: monitor provider licensing, insurance, approved vendor status, and scheduling blocks
user_stories.outcome: providers that do not meet required compliance conditions are flagged before work is scheduled or approved

acceptance_criteria.description: Provider qualifications are tracked with effective dates
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A Service Provider record can store Provider Qualification entries for license, insurance, bonding, certification, and approved vendor status, each with effective date and expiration date when applicable

acceptance_criteria.description: Scheduling blocks are enforced
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: If a provider linked to a case has an expired or missing required qualification, the system prevents creation of a schedulable appointment state and records the blocking reason on the case or provider record

acceptance_criteria.description: Compliance alerts are generated for changes in status
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: When a tracked provider qualification expires or changes to a non-compliant status, the system creates an alert or task linked to the provider within 1 workflow run and makes the blocking condition visible to staff

user_stories.action: track operational billing and payment outcomes under a configurable Phase 1 business model
user_stories.outcome: the platform can record who owes what and why for homeowner, provider, or concierge-side charges without hard-coding a single monetization model

acceptance_criteria.description: Billing model is configurable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The platform stores one active billing model per tenant that can assign billable responsibility to homeowner, service provider, staff-assisted concierge service, or a supported combination

acceptance_criteria.description: Charges and invoices retain operational context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Each Charge or Invoice created under the active billing model is linked to the related property, service case, provider, or community record that caused the charge

acceptance_criteria.description: Payments are allocatable and auditable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: A recorded Payment can be allocated to one or more invoice line items or charges through Payment Allocation records, and the full allocation history remains retrievable for audit

user_stories.action: define and run product-level operational reports for each pillar phase
user_stories.outcome: success outcomes are measured through explicit report definitions instead of broad implementation claims

acceptance_criteria.description: Success outcomes are captured as saved reporting definitions
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: An operations leader can create one or more Report Definition records that specify metric name, source scope, date range logic, and owning phase or pillar

acceptance_criteria.description: Report runs produce dated evidence
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Executing a saved report creates a Report Run with generation timestamp and stored metric outputs for the selected scope

acceptance_criteria.description: Metrics can be compared across reporting periods
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: The system can display at least two KPI Snapshot values for the same metric across different reporting dates for the same property, portfolio, community, provider, or workflow scope
